zakatClaimEvidence: The Muslim Community Center Chicago explicitly accepts zakat donations, as evidenced by their dedicated 'Rehab & Welfare/ Zakat Request' section on their website, which details the application process for zakat distribution and mentions 'In person applications or Zakat distribution will only be every Thursday & Saturday: 11am -5 pm. Online applications will continue 7 days a week'. They also provide an 'Online Zakat Form' for financial assistance, stating, 'All applications are approved or denied at the sole discretion of The Muslim Community Center of Chicago.' Furthermore, 'Zakat Request' is listed as one of their offered services. (Source: https://mccchicago.org)

all_citations.claim: The organization reported total revenue of $10,751,849, a program expense ratio of 93.4%, and 23.6 months of working capital.
Financial metadata confirms a fundraising efficiency of $0.03 per $1 raised.
The organization maintains active community governance and operates programs including the MCC Academy and religious services.
The organization is Zakat-eligible and provides various giving methods for donors.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a moderate overall score due to its exceptional financial efficiency and deep community roots, balanced by a need for more robust external impact data.
areas_for_improvement: Enhancing public transparency regarding specific program outcomes and impact metrics.
Increasing data confidence through more frequent third-party evaluations.

dimension_explanations.alignment: MCC shows high alignment for Muslim donors as a Zakat-eligible institution that addresses urgent religious and cultural needs through its mosque and academy [3][4]. Its long-standing track record in the Chicago area establishes it as a primary hub for communal and spiritual life [3].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization demonstrates a capacity-building approach by maintaining a 93.4% program expense ratio and providing essential religious infrastructure [1][2]. While financial health is robust with 23.6 months of working capital, more detailed evidence of long-term beneficiary outcomes is needed to reach the highest impact tiers [1].

baseline_narrative.headline: The Muslim Community Center Incorporate serves as a cornerstone of Chicago's Muslim community through comprehensive religious services and high-impact educational programming.
strengths: High program expense ratio of 93.4%, ensuring the vast majority of funds directly support community services.
Exceptional fundraising efficiency with a cost of only $0.03 per $1 raised.
Strong liquidity position with 23.6 months of working capital available.
baseline_narrative.summary: Based in Chicago, the Muslim Community Center (MCC) provides a wide range of services including the teaching and recitation of the Holy Quran, funeral and marriage services, and daily congregations [1][3]. The organization operates the MCC Academy and various adult education programs, supported by a total revenue of $10,751,849 [1][2].

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who prioritize supporting established Islamic community infrastructure and accredited religious education in the American Midwest.
donor_motivations: Want to support a multi-generational community cornerstone
Value high financial efficiency and low fundraising costs
Seek to fulfill zakat through a variety of eligible categories
Interested in supporting both local services and domestic grantmaking
giving_considerations: Donations support a large, established institution with significant assets
Impact is measured more through institutional longevity than specific outcome data
High liquidity suggests the organization is not in an immediate financial crisis
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking high-frequency impact reporting or those looking to fund international relief directly rather than through a community hub.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects verified program delivery and accreditation, but a lack of rigorous third-party impact evaluations.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: MCC creates impact by providing a centralized hub for religious, educational, and social services, fostering community cohesion and spiritual growth.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that the high volume of program spending translates into measurable community improvements.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: While MCC has strong reserves, its <cite id="13">17.8% revenue growth</cite> indicates expanding community needs and program capacity.

strengths.detail: The organization directs <cite id="7">93.4% of its expenses toward programs</cite> and maintains a low fundraising cost of <cite id="8">$0.03 per $1 raised</cite>.
MCC maintains <cite id="11">23.6 months of working capital</cite>, providing a significant buffer for long-term operational sustainability.
Operating for <cite id="1">over 55 years</cite>, the center serves multiple generations through <cite id="3">accredited full-time schooling and religious services</cite>.

strengths_deep_dive: MCC's financial health is characterized by a high degree of stability and efficient resource allocation. With <cite id="12">total assets of $19,394,203</cite> and a <cite id="13">3-year revenue CAGR of 17.8%</cite>, the organization is in a strong growth phase. Its grantmaking activity is particularly notable, providing significant funding to partners like <cite id="14">Medglobal Inc ($75,000)</cite> and <cite id="15">Helping Hand For Relief & Development ($41,123)</cite>, demonstrating a commitment to broader humanitarian causes beyond its local Chicago footprint.
rich_narrative.summary: Founded in <cite id="1">1969</cite>, the Muslim Community Center (MCC) has established itself as a foundational institution for the Chicago Muslim community, operating across locations in <cite id="2">Chicago, Morton Grove, and Skokie</cite>. The organization manages a diverse portfolio of services including <cite id="3">religious services, funeral and marriage rites</cite>, and the highly-regarded <cite id="4">MCC Academy</cite>. Financially, the organization demonstrates significant scale with <cite id="5">$9,945,910 in total revenue</cite> for the 2023 tax year, primarily driven by <cite id="6">$6,039,439 in program service revenue</cite>. MCC maintains an exceptionally lean financial profile, reporting a <cite id="7">93.4% program expense ratio</cite> and a fundraising efficiency of <cite id="8">$0.03 per $1 raised</cite>. Beyond local services, MCC acts as a philanthropic hub, distributing <cite id="9">$714,141 in domestic grants</cite> to various humanitarian and community partners. This dual role as both a direct service provider and a grantmaker allows MCC to address immediate spiritual needs while supporting broader <cite id="10">humanitarian relief efforts</cite> through established partners.
